phase-scope.phase-32: Domain, HTTPS redirect, wildcard TLS, and shared 443 routing verification for krotpn.xyz passed local/static gates; live smoke remains operator-required.
phase-scope.phase-34: Stabilization gate reuses local/static edge checks and keeps production DNS, wildcard TLS, shared 443, and real Telegram client smoke as explicit operator-live evidence.
phase-scope.phase-35: Approved installer wildcard TLS changes provide deploy-supported operator certificate reuse and runtime nginx domain rendering while preserving shared-443 live gates.
phase-scope.phase-37: Shared 443 is implemented by mtproto-edge owning public TCP 443 and forwarding normal HTTPS to nginx fallback on 8443.
phase-scope.phase-38: Shared 443 is redefined as RU SNI router ownership with web SNI to RU nginx fallback and issued MTProto SNI to the DE runtime.

commands.command-1: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_domain_tls_edge_static.py -v
commands.command-2: sh -c 'if [ -e .env ]; then DB_PASSWORD=phase32-compose-check docker compose config; else ln -s .env.example .env && trap "rm -f .env" EXIT && DB_PASSWORD=phase32-compose-check docker compose config; fi'
commands.command-4: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-32.xml docs/modules/M-046.xml docs/verification/V-M-046.xml docs/verification-index.xml && mygrace lint
commands.command-6: operator-live smoke after DNS and wildcard certificate readiness: HTTP redirect, HTTPS frontend/API/admin health through 8443 fallback, issued MTProto SNI route, Telegram Desktop available status, rollback rehearsal notes.

verification-levels.module-local: M-046 static tests prove krotpn.xyz domain settings, redirect rules, wildcard certificate paths, single 443 owner contract, and protected deploy/install script boundaries.
verification-levels.wave-level: M-012 compose validation and M-044 runtime bridge tests remain in scope so shared 443 does not break existing deploy composition or MTProto policy replay.
verification-levels.phase-level: Production-like smoke proves real DNS, trusted wildcard TLS, HTTPS fallback, and at least one issued MTProto SNI before public advertisement.

module-checks.check-1: M-001 settings expose canonical krotpn.xyz frontend/domain values, MTProto base domain krotpn.xyz, and proxy port 443 without logging secrets.
module-checks.check-2: HTTP requests for krotpn.xyz and www.krotpn.xyz permanently redirect to https://krotpn.xyz without serving mixed HTTP content.
module-checks.check-3: TLS configuration expects trusted wildcard coverage for krotpn.xyz and *.krotpn.xyz and does not accept self-signed certs for production edge claims.
module-checks.check-4: Docker compose resolves one explicit owner for public 443, avoiding simultaneous nginx and MTProto bind conflicts.
module-checks.check-5: Normal HTTPS fallback keeps frontend, /api, and /admin routes reachable with X-Forwarded-Proto=https semantics.
module-checks.check-6: MTProto SNI routing accepts only issued user SNI names from the M-044 runtime policy and never exposes base secrets or derived secrets in traces.
module-checks.check-7: Phase-32/34 verification fails if deploy scripts or install.sh drift outside explicitly approved Phase-35 wildcard TLS installer scope.
module-checks.check-8: Rollback evidence shows how to restore previous nginx-only 443 behavior if shared edge routing fails.
module-checks.check-9: Phase-34 cannot claim production readiness until HTTPS, admin, API, one issued MTProto SNI, unknown SNI behavior, and rollback readiness are live-smoked.
module-checks.check-10: nginx no longer binds listen 443 ssl; mtproto-edge is the public 443 owner and nginx HTTPS fallback listens on 8443.
module-checks.check-11: RU SNI router is the only public TCP 443 owner, web SNI routes to RU nginx 8443, and MTProto SNI routes to DE runtime.
module-checks.check-12: Rollback path restores nginx-only public 443 before any failed Telegram retest is advertised to users.

phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-1: Local execution may run static edge checks only; no DNS, certificate, nginx, docker-compose, deploy, install, or live server changes are allowed without operator scope.
phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-2: Release handoff must record whether live shared 443 smoke passed, remains blocked, or is explicitly waived with risk ownership.

scenarios.scenario-1: http://krotpn.xyz and http://www.krotpn.xyz redirect permanently to https://krotpn.xyz.
scenarios.scenario-2: https://krotpn.xyz serves the user frontend and https://krotpn.xyz/api traffic reaches the backend through the HTTPS fallback route.
scenarios.scenario-3: https://krotpn.xyz/admin remains reachable for the admin frontend after shared 443 changes.
scenarios.scenario-4: An issued MTProto fake-TLS SNI under *.krotpn.xyz routes to the MTProto runtime bridge without changing the user's VPN state.
scenarios.scenario-5: Unknown SNI, expired wildcard cert, missing cert files, or DNS mismatch fail closed and do not leak proxy secrets.
scenarios.scenario-6: 443 bind conflict or bad shared-edge classification triggers rollback to the previous nginx-only public surface.
scenarios.scenario-7: RU SNI router forwards browser HTTPS to RU nginx fallback and Telegram fake-TLS to DE runtime without exposing policy API publicly.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Verification logs must never print certificate private keys, .env values, MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, derived secrets, tg://proxy links, JWTs, or email verification tokens.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Live smoke output may include hostnames, status codes, certificate issuer/expiry, route names, and redacted SNI suffixes only.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Admin and diagnostic summaries must not include full per-user MTProto links or raw fake-TLS secrets.

live-smoke-gates.gate-1: DNS A or AAAA for krotpn.xyz and wildcard SNI test names points to the intended edge host before live smoke.
live-smoke-gates.gate-2: Let's Encrypt or another trusted certificate validates krotpn.xyz and *.krotpn.xyz without browser or curl trust bypass.
live-smoke-gates.gate-3: HTTP redirect returns a permanent redirect to the canonical HTTPS host.
live-smoke-gates.gate-4: HTTPS frontend, backend API health, and admin route return healthy responses through the public edge.
live-smoke-gates.gate-5: One issued MTProto SNI reaches the runtime bridge while unknown SNI remains safely rejected or falls back to HTTPS as designed.
live-smoke-gates.gate-6: Rollback procedure is documented and rehearsed enough to restore previous public behavior quickly.
live-smoke-gates.gate-7: Phase-37 live smoke confirms public TCP 443 owner, nginx 8443 fallback owner, local policy health, public HTTPS response, and Telegram Desktop proxy availability.
live-smoke-gates.gate-8: Phase-38 live smoke confirms RU SNI router public 443 owner, DE runtime target, private policy health, public HTTPS response, and Telegram Desktop proxy availability.

telemetry-requirements.metric-1: edge_http_redirect_count tagged by host and status code.
telemetry-requirements.metric-2: edge_sni_classification_count tagged by route_type without secret-bearing labels.
telemetry-requirements.metric-3: edge_https_fallback_count tagged by route frontend, api, or admin.
telemetry-requirements.metric-4: edge_mtproto_route_count tagged by issued/unissued classification only.
telemetry-requirements.metric-5: edge_rollback_trigger_count tagged by safe failure code.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: Static tests, docker compose config, XML parsing, MyGRACE lint, and secret-redaction checks are safe to run locally.
autonomous-execution-safety.requires-operator: Live DNS, wildcard certificate issuance, public 443 ownership changes, production smoke, and Telegram Desktop proof require explicit operator readiness and rollback window.

evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-14: docker compose config passed using a temporary .env symlink to .env.example and DB_PASSWORD=phase32-compose-check.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-14: scripts/phase32-edge-contract-smoke.mjs passed and protected deploy/install scripts were not changed.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-14: M-044/M-043/M-040 regression pytest passed locally with 29 tests for runtime bridge, scheduler, MTProto provisioning, email delivery, and email verification guards.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-14: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and git diff whitespace checks passed.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-14: Phase-34 handoff records V-M-046 live smoke as operator-required and the local static smoke keeps deploy/install/edge surfaces unchanged.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-17: Phase-35 updated the edge static guard to allow only approved M-048 installer wildcard TLS deploy surface changes; M-046 public shared-443 live smoke remains operator-required.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-05-18: Phase-37 corrected the live design so mtproto-edge owns public 443 and normal HTTPS fallback moves to nginx 8443.
evidence.evidence-9: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 verification redefines the public 443 live gate around RU SNI router plus DE runtime after RU-only mtproto_proxy stayed blocked-live.
evidence.evidence-10: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 local execution passed SNI router static coverage, compose public-443 ownership checks, nginx HTTPS fallback checks, and docker compose config with .env.example.
